tweets.text:
$BB earnings is coming up and we are entering the potential dip zone I had outlined 2 weeks ago. I'll be a buyer this next week

All the signs so far are hinting at a pretty strong earnings, at least from previous public statements and guidance in terms of growth. So the moment of truth is here. 

My plan is to hope to end up with some free shares. The portion that I buy, I will likely sell if we have a strong rally into earnings, and after. Then keep the net difference at 0 cost. Just fyi

thread_tweets.text:
fwiw my entry was 5.79 

but this just means you've not done any dd

CEO- no longer a company in transition, now a profitable growth comapny phase. 5% stock buyback. Stated rule of 40. In 275m vehichles, will be in robotics already being integrated and is becoming crucial, defense, and medical applications. Secure communications being a “digital sovereignty tailwind” governments wanting non-us dependent secure comms. they just showed off a QNX powered humanoid demo. + nvda parntership integrated with THOR/halo safety stack. Weekly calls with NVDA as heavily integrated 

Miltiary contracts, being used in submarines being built in the EU 

A tech stack that can't be rebuilt. Would take a decade + over 10B 

They already have a huge sticky growing base in automotive which is going to expand even more rapidly than it already is. +10% rev grwoth last year, 78% margins. 950m QNX backlog

434m in cash and investments end of last year
232m net cash

the cybersecurity portion did 72.5m last q 

qnx is the big growth we are looking at this q

tweets.text:
of course there have been liquidations, but there’s always going to be liquidations whenever there’s a violent repricing

the question isn’t “why were there liquidations”, those are natural and expected. the question is why was there a violent repricing

your emphasis on the mechanics of liquidations is arguably distracting from the more important, fundamental repricing that occurred

i can share my hypothesis for why the repricing happened but i think you know what it is already

thread_tweets.text:
yeah we largely agree but maybe not on everything

i don’t think that SATA’s relative stability has all that much to do with the daily dividends. that’s part of the story but not the main story imo

imo SATA is generally more stable because it’s simply much smaller and therefore safer. at $750m outstanding, there’s all kinds of ways to stabilize it and therefore it is less risky

STRC is $10b outstanding and that’s an entirely different ball game. the fact that on paper strategy has ~5x more btc than STRC outstanding (vs strive’s ~1.6x) isn’t really helping here because the nominal amount is so big

i do think if saylor does decide to sell multi-billion worth of BTC it’ll put very serious pressure on BTC price, which would put a lot of pressure on strive’s BTC-to-prefs ratio which is currently ~1.6x, hence putting serious pressure on SATA price itself

and that’s the real reason why SATA reprices lower whenever STRC dislocates. they are fundamentally tied together at the hip because when STRC goes down it increases the odds of saylor selling large amounts of BTC, which would in turn add a lot of risk to SATA

tweets.text:
IDK who needs to hear this, but:

• NFA = Please don’t sue me

• Zoom out = Daily chart is cooked

• I'm derisking = I got liquidated

• Sources tell me = Read it in a TG gc

• Great entry here = I need exit liquidity

• Taking profits soon = I will take a screenshot

• Market inefficiency = RSI dropped below 20

• Institutions are coming = I’m coping

• Great risk/reward = Great risk and no reward

• Strong fundamentals = Price is down -90%

• Strong support zone = My liquidation price zone

Don't thank me.

tweets.text:
personal lore episode 2

2019

did this project for Oculus
pushed the limits of immersive content 

piece was directed by my bros
michael fullman (acronym) and @gmunk 

“make people experience VR
but without the headset”

360 architectural projection mapping
complementing lasers, lights, holoscrim, fx 

lead the team at Possible
who developed the projections
in collaboration with industrial light & magic 
for some of the star wars bits

pretty tough one to pull off
hard to beat IRL events
A+ team

would later apply the practical learnings when I became an advisor for Sphere in Vegas

here’s a snippet
tweets.full_text:
personal lore episode 2

2019

did this project for Oculus
pushed the limits of immersive content 

piece was directed by my bros
michael fullman (acronym) and @gmunk 

“make people experience VR
but without the headset”

360 architectural projection mapping
complementing lasers, lights, holoscrim, fx 

lead the team at Possible
who developed the projections
in collaboration with industrial light & magic 
for some of the star wars bits

pretty tough one to pull off
hard to beat IRL events
A+ team

would later apply the practical learnings when I became an advisor for Sphere in Vegas

here’s a snippet

tweets.text:
largely agree

that’s why you see us empowering builders that are engaged (recently @Escapation and @tropicalvirtual)

think people incorrectly imagine that some big AAA developer will step in and make the next big thing on any ugc platform

but that has never happened

not on warcraft 3
not on roblox

it’s always the native developers
the ones born from within the community
who figure it out

guinsoo and icefrog didn’t create dota because of a contest blizzard put on

grow a garden was not a byproduct of an official contest, bmwlux shipped it for the love of the game

as we continue to make progress on otherside more people will be inspired to build in it

thread_tweets.text:
limited access isn’t a feature to feign scarcity - anyone with an otherdeed can reach out to @tropicalvirtual or @pm0721 to get access to the odk 

instead, current access is more a factor of finite development resources. team is aiming focus at otherdeeds/resource game/live ops to make sure those foundations are strong before toggling to developer support on what could be a vastly more diverse problem set

to your other point - chasing game engine updates can be a bit of a trap, better to stick to a version and build. first and second trip were on unreal engine 4 and that didn’t bother anyone

tweets.text:
🚀 CleanTech Vanadium ( $CTV.V | $CTVFF ) is moving from planning to execution.
The company has launched a major drill program at its Campbell-Crotser #fluorspar project in Kentucky, targeting material for an initial underground mine plan and a planned 500 tpd processing operation.

With fluorspar designated as a critical mineral and a refreshed website now live, CleanTech continues to build its U.S. critical minerals platform.

Worth a look: https://t.co/duqHL5q2TG

Disclosure: Paid partnership with CleanTech Vanadium. Not financial advice. Always do your own due diligence.

🚀 CleanTech Vanadium ( $CTV.V | $CTVFF ) is moving from planning to execution.
The company has launched a major drill program at its Campbell-Crotser #fluorspar project in Kentucky, targeting material for an initial underground mine plan and a planned 500 tpd processing operation.

With fluorspar designated as a critical mineral and a refreshed website now live, CleanTech continues to build its U.S. critical minerals platform.

Worth a look: https://t.co/duqHL5q2TG

Disclosure: Paid partnership with CleanTech Vanadium. Not financial advice. Always do your own due diligence.

Evolving into the perfectly positioned barbell balanced ecosystem right now.

And this right at the market bottom.

Here's the truth: the people panicking about AI taking jobs are mostly mourning jobs humans never should have been stuck doing! Did anyone dream of being in a call-center queue? 

We are freeing people from the interesting problems we face!

tweets.text: If we all think alike the world would be boring. I think Saylor’s financial innovations will change the world (it has only just started, STRC’s issues are just growing pains), but I also understand that people like Tim see him as a threat to BTC. I think in the long run most will realise Saylor was a visionary, long before the world was ready for that.

tweets.text:
$STRC is an asset that is supposed to remain in a $99-$100 range while being backed by an asset that regularly moves +/-25% in a month

That is fundamentally impossible

But on the flip side, having a pref pegged at $100 makes it infinitely easier to grasp for retail

tweets.text:
This is honestly one of the best written Substacks I've read in a while.  Joe really nails the current hypocrisy of institutions in a 4th Turning and the new institutions that will be built in the 1st Turning using new mental models.  It's really worth your time to understand why our very sclerotic institutions are undermining our very civilizational interests.

From @JTLonsdale @AmOptimistShow :

"Robert Conquest’s third law of politics is as follows:

'The behavior of any bureaucratic organization can best be understood by assuming that it is controlled by a secret cabal of its enemies.'

That line has always sounded funny because it is exaggerated; it keeps being useful because, in case after case, it is not exaggerated enough. 

Universities that are supposed to cultivate excellence and bring up the most talented young people in our society as leaders instead teach them contempt for our history and values. 

Charities that were created to help the needy have reoriented themselves almost entirely to help the overpaid people who run them (and often, their friends & political allies, too)..."

https://t.co/TMP9jiDgPV

@millerman
